clusters.title: Trump touts business wins as China airs Iran, Taiwan concerns
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump and Xi hold high-stakes talks in Beijing
bullet_summary: The pomp and warm words during Trump’s visit were overshadowed by Xi’s blunt warning that US missteps on Taiwan could push the two countries into conflict, Chinese media reported
Xi’s remarks, published by China’s foreign ministry after his two-hour meeting with Trump on Thursday morning, said Taiwan was “the most important issue in China-US relations”

items.title: Trump touts business wins as China airs Iran, Taiwan concerns
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump entered his final talks with Xi ​Jinping on Friday touting economic wins that gave markets little to cheer, while Beijing warned Washington about mishandling Taiwan and said its war with Iran should never have started. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington DC.
items.content: US President Donald Trump entered his final talks with Xi ​Jinping on Friday touting economic wins that gave markets little to cheer, while Beijing warned Washington about mishandling Taiwan and said its war with Iran should never have started. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington DC.

items.title: Trump leaves Beijing with few wins, many warm words for Xi
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump departed China on Friday ​touting business deals that gave markets little to cheer, while Beijing warned Washington about mishandling Taiwan and said its war with Iran should never have started. Trump's visit to America's main strategic and economic rival, the first by a US president since his last trip in 2017, had aimed for tangible results to beef up his dented approval ratings ahead of crucial midterm elections.
items.content: US President Donald Trump departed China on Friday ​touting business deals that gave markets little to cheer, while Beijing warned Washington about mishandling Taiwan and said its war with Iran should never have started. Trump's visit to America's main strategic and economic rival, the first by a US president since his last trip in 2017, had aimed for tangible results to beef up his dented approval ratings ahead of crucial midterm elections.

items.title: Trump touts "fantastic trade deals" with Xi as Beijing summit ends
items.standfirst: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping concluded their summit in Beijing on Friday with both countries looking to claim the visit as a win.
items.content: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping concluded their summit in Beijing on Friday with both countries looking to claim the visit as a win.

items.title: Trump and Xi hold high-stakes talks in Beijing
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are meeting for a second day in Beijing.
items.content: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are meeting for a second day in Beijing.

items.title: Trump hails ‘fantastic trade deals’ with Xi, China issues warning on Taiwan
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump touted economic wins ahead of his final talks with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping on Friday, claiming the two made “fantastic trade deals”. The pomp and warm words during Trump’s visit were overshadowed by Xi’s blunt warning that US missteps on Taiwan could push the two countries into conflict, Chinese media reported.
items.content: President Donald Trump touted economic wins ahead of his final talks with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping on Friday, claiming the two made “fantastic trade deals”. The pomp and warm words during Trump’s visit were overshadowed by Xi’s blunt warning that US missteps on Taiwan could push the two countries into conflict, Chinese media reported.

items.title: Families of Americans imprisoned in China appeal to Trump for help
items.standfirst: After living without their loved ones for more than a decade, two families of Americans detained in China are appealing to President Donald Trump to bring them home as he meets with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing.
items.content: After living without their loved ones for more than a decade, two families of Americans detained in China are appealing to President Donald Trump to bring them home as he meets with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing.

items.title: For anxious Taiwan, Trump’s silence after Xi talks is best possible outcome
items.standfirst: Island breathes sigh of relief as fears recede that US could jettison longstanding supportBefore this week’s summit between the Chinese and US presidents, Taiwan had been cast as the anxious bystander.Observers suggested that Taipei feared the unpredictable and transactional Donald Trump might overturn Washington’s longstanding support for the island democracy, which Beijing claims as a breakaway province, during Thursday and Friday’s talks. Continue reading...
items.content: Island breathes sigh of relief as fears recede that US could jettison longstanding supportBefore this week’s summit between the Chinese and US presidents, Taiwan had been cast as the anxious bystander.Observers suggested that Taipei feared the unpredictable and transactional Donald Trump might overturn Washington’s longstanding support for the island democracy, which Beijing claims as a breakaway province, during Thursday and Friday’s talks. Continue reading...

items.title: Xi warns Trump of possible conflict over Taiwan at grand Beijing summit
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned of “clashes and even conflicts” with the U.S. over Taiwan, cautioning President Donald Trump that tensions over the Beijing-claimed island could jeopardize ties between the world’s two biggest economies.
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned of “clashes and even conflicts” with the U.S. over Taiwan, cautioning President Donald Trump that tensions over the Beijing-claimed island could jeopardize ties between the world’s two biggest economies.

clusters.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with Raul Castro's grandson in Havana, US and Cuban officials say
clusters.neutral_headline: CIA director has met officials in Havana for talks, Cuba claims
bullet_summary: The meeting took place “in a context marked by the complexity of bilateral relations, with the aim of contributing to the political dialogue between both nations”, a statement said
CIA chief John Ratcliffe met Cuban officials in Havana on Thursday in a bid to improve dialogue between Washington and the communist-run island, the Cuban government said
The meeting took place “in a context marked by the complexity of bilateral relations", Havana said

items.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with Raul Castro's grandson in Havana, US and Cuban officials say
items.standfirst: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with Cuban officials including Raúl Castro's grandson during a high-level visit to the island Thursday, Cuban and U.S. officials said.
items.content: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with Cuban officials including Raúl Castro's grandson during a high-level visit to the island Thursday, Cuban and U.S. officials said.(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)

clusters.title: Seized ship taken toward Iran as Trump and Xi agree on Strait of Hormuz

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, NPR News, Guardian World

items.title: Seized ship taken toward Iran as Trump and Xi agree on Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: A ship was taken by unknown parties toward Iranian waters after an Indian-flagged vessel was attacked off Oman.
items.content: A ship was taken by unknown parties toward Iranian waters after an Indian-flagged vessel was attacked off Oman.

items.title: Tensions flare near Strait of Hormuz as a ship is seized and another is sunk
items.standfirst: Tensions are escalating again near the Strait of Hormuz after a ship anchored off the United Arab Emirates was seized and taken toward Iran and another was attacked and sank near the coast of Oman.
items.content: Tensions are escalating again near the Strait of Hormuz after a ship anchored off the United Arab Emirates was seized and taken toward Iran and another was attacked and sank near the coast of Oman.(Image credit: Digital Embed)

items.title: Iran says ships entering strait of Hormuz must cooperate after vessel seized
items.standfirst: Docked ship reportedly seized outside UAE port by “unauthorised personnel”The Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, has said ships entering the strait of Hormuz must cooperate with the Iranian navy as reports emerged of a ship being seized outside a United Arab Emirate port and taken towards Iranian waters.The UK Maritime Trading Organisation said the docked ship was seized by “unauthorised personnel” while it was anchored off the coast of the United Arab Emirates port of Fujairah near the southern entry to the strait of Hormuz. Continue reading...
items.content: Docked ship reportedly seized outside UAE port by “unauthorised personnel”The Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, has said ships entering the strait of Hormuz must cooperate with the Iranian navy as reports emerged of a ship being seized outside a United Arab Emirate port and taken towards Iranian waters.The UK Maritime Trading Organisation said the docked ship was seized by “unauthorised personnel” while it was anchored off the coast of the United Arab Emirates port of Fujairah near the southern entry to the strait of Hormuz. clusters.title: Abu Dhabi 'doesn't want to appear too much in cooperation with Israel', analyst says
clusters.neutral_headline: Benjamin Netanyahu says he made secret trip to UAE at height of Iran war
bullet_summary: Earlier, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had announced he had quietly visited the UAE during the Israeli-US war with Iran, though the UAE swiftly denied that any secret visit had occurred
“This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said on Wednesday night
Benjamin Netanyahu's office said he secretly visited the United Arab Emirates earlier this year during the US-Israeli war with Iran - but the UAE has denied the trip took place

items.title: Abu Dhabi 'doesn't want to appear too much in cooperation with Israel', analyst says
items.standfirst: A renewed escalation in the Strait of Hormuz saw the seizure on Thursday of a ship anchored off the Unites Arab Emirates coast. Earlier, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had announced he had quietly visited the UAE during the Israeli-US war with Iran, though the UAE swiftly denied that any secret visit had occurred. Speaking on FRANCE 24, Federico Donelli, Assistant Professor of International Relations at the University of Trieste, says that "Abu Dhabi does not want to appear too much in cooperation with Israel in the face of what Israel is doing in the region".
items.content: A renewed escalation in the Strait of Hormuz saw the seizure on Thursday of a ship anchored off the Unites Arab Emirates coast. Earlier, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had announced he had quietly visited the UAE during the Israeli-US war with Iran, though the UAE swiftly denied that any secret visit had occurred. Speaking on FRANCE 24, Federico Donelli, Assistant Professor of International Relations at the University of Trieste, says that "Abu Dhabi does not want to appear too much in cooperation with Israel in the face of what Israel is doing in the region".

items.title: Benjamin Netanyahu says he made secret trip to UAE at height of Iran war
items.standfirst: Emirates’ foreign ministry rejects claims that Netanyahu visited country, describing them as ‘baseless’Benjamin Netanyahu has claimed he made a secret trip to the United Arab Emirates at the height of the Iran war to meet the president, Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan.“This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said on Wednesday night. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Israelis chant threats, anti-Palestinian slogans at Jerusalem Day march
clusters.neutral_headline: Israeli nationalists chant ‘death to Arabs’ in violent Jerusalem Day march

items.title: Israelis chant threats, anti-Palestinian slogans at Jerusalem Day march
items.standfirst: Israeli nationalists swept through the narrow streets of Jerusalem's Old City on Thursday, chanting "Death to Arabs" during the annual Jerusalem Day march, while many Palestinian residents remained barricaded indoors. Every year, tens of thousands of Israelis celebrate what Israeli authorities call the "reunification" of Jerusalem following Israel's annexation of east Jerusalem in the 1967 Arab-Israeli war. The annexation of east Jerusalem, home to a predominantly Palestinian population, is not recognised by the United Nations, which considers it illegal under international law.
items.content: Israeli nationalists swept through the narrow streets of Jerusalem's Old City on Thursday, chanting "Death to Arabs" during the annual Jerusalem Day march, while many Palestinian residents remained barricaded indoors. Every year, tens of thousands of Israelis celebrate what Israeli authorities call the "reunification" of Jerusalem following Israel's annexation of east Jerusalem in the 1967 Arab-Israeli war. The annexation of east Jerusalem, home to a predominantly Palestinian population, is not recognised by the United Nations, which considers it illegal under international law.

items.title: Israeli nationalists chant ‘death to Arabs’ in violent Jerusalem Day march
items.standfirst: Far-right Jewish marchers call for Palestinian villages to ‘burn’ as they storm through Muslim quarter of Old CityIsraeli nationalists chanted “death to the Arabs”, “may your villages burn” and “Gaza is a graveyard” in a state-sponsored march through Jerusalem to mark the anniversary of the city’s capture and annexation.The annual assertion of Jewish control over Palestinian East Jerusalem has grown more extreme in recent years, and Thursday’s event culminated with the national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, unfurling an Israeli flag in front of the al-Aqsa mosque, the holiest Islamic site in the city. Continue reading...
items.content: Far-right Jewish marchers call for Palestinian villages to ‘burn’ as they storm through Muslim quarter of Old CityIsraeli nationalists chanted “death to the Arabs”, “may your villages burn” and “Gaza is a graveyard” in a state-sponsored march through Jerusalem to mark the anniversary of the city’s capture and annexation.The annual assertion of Jewish control over Palestinian East Jerusalem has grown more extreme in recent years, and Thursday’s event culminated with the national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, unfurling an Israeli flag in front of the al-Aqsa mosque, the holiest Islamic site in the city. Continue reading...

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC Top Stories, Guardian World

items.title: British Gas pays £20m over prepayment meter force-fitting scandal
items.standfirst: The regulator say the energy supplier breached licence conditions aimed at protecting customers in vulnerable situations.
items.content: The regulator say the energy supplier breached licence conditions aimed at protecting customers in vulnerable situations.

items.title: British Gas customers to receive up to £112m over prepayment meter scandal
items.standfirst: Redress for force-fitting devices in homes includes £20m penalty and £70m of debt write-offsThousands of British Gas customers who had prepayment meters force-fitted in their homes will between them receive compensation and energy bill debt write-offs worth up to £112m in the biggest energy supplier settlement on record.Great Britain’s energy regulator found that British Gas forced PPMs on homes that were not keeping up with their bills at the height of the Russian gas crisis, in one of the most complex investigations in Ofgem’s history. Continue reading...
items.content: Redress for force-fitting devices in homes includes £20m penalty and £70m of debt write-offsThousands of British Gas customers who had prepayment meters force-fitted in their homes will between them receive compensation and energy bill debt write-offs worth up to £112m in the biggest energy supplier settlement on record.Great Britain’s energy regulator found that British Gas forced PPMs on homes that were not keeping up with their bills at the height of the Russian gas crisis, in one of the most complex investigations in Ofgem’s history. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Colossal dinosaur unearthed in Thailand weighs as much as nine elephants
clusters.neutral_headline: Giant new dinosaur identified from fossils in Thailand
bullet_summary: Scientists estimate the long-necked herbivore weighed as much as nine adult Asian elephants and is the largest ever found in Southeast Asia
The nagatitan is the largest dinosaur found in South-East Asia and weighs as much as nine elephants

items.title: Colossal dinosaur unearthed in Thailand weighs as much as nine elephants
items.standfirst: The remains of a previously unknown dinosaur species, dubbed Nagatitan chaiyaphumensis, has been excavated in Thailand, according to a study published in the journal Scientific Reports. Scientists estimate the long-necked herbivore weighed as much as nine adult Asian elephants and is the largest ever found in Southeast Asia.
items.content: The remains of a previously unknown dinosaur species, dubbed Nagatitan chaiyaphumensis, has been excavated in Thailand, according to a study published in the journal Scientific Reports. Scientists estimate the long-necked herbivore weighed as much as nine adult Asian elephants and is the largest ever found in Southeast Asia.

items.title: Giant new dinosaur identified from fossils in Thailand
items.standfirst: The nagatitan is the largest dinosaur found in South-East Asia and weighs as much as nine elephants.
items.content: The nagatitan is the largest dinosaur found in South-East Asia and weighs as much as nine elephants.

clusters.title: Supreme Court upholds mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now
clusters.neutral_headline: Supreme Court upholds mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Supreme Court upholds mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court has maintained mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone, setting aside for now a lower court order that blocked abortion providers from prescribing the widely used drug through telehealth and shipping it to patients.
items.content: The Supreme Court has maintained mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone, setting aside for now a lower court order that blocked abortion providers from prescribing the widely used drug through telehealth and shipping it to patients.

items.title: Supreme court allows abortion pill mifepristone to continue to be available by mail
items.standfirst: Louisiana had sued the FDA in a bid to curtail the regulatory agency’s rules on prescribing mifepristone remotelySign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe US supreme court upheld nationwide access to mail-order mifepristone, an abortion medication, in a shadow-docket decision on Thursday.Louisiana sued the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in October in a bid to curtail the regulatory agency’s rules on prescribing mifepristone remotely, arguing that it interfered with the state’s ban on abortion. Continue reading...
